question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted StreamFileSignature whose getNode().getTotalStake() returns a near-zero value as the first element in the collection, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute an artificially low threshold, so that a minority of tampered signatures passes consensus and a fraudulent transaction record is accepted as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where the first element has totalStake=1 but subsequent elements have totalStake=1000000, exploiting the fact that totalStake is only read from the first signature (line 45), so that stakeRequiredForConsensus is computed from the wrong denominator, allowing a tampered transaction stream file to reach false consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature with a null fileHash such that getFileHashAsHex() returns null, causing signatureHashMap.put(null, signature) to insert a null key into the HashMultimap, and then a second tampered signature also with null fileHash groups with it, allowing a fraudulent file hash to reach consensus and a tampered transaction record to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio()) uses value-and-scale equality (not compareTo), so that a consensusRatio of new BigDecimal('0.0') with a different scale bypasses the zero-ratio early-return check at line 54, causing the validator to skip consensus and accept any tampered transaction file without threshold verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection where all StreamFileSignature objects have status DOWNLOADED (not VERIFIED), so that signatureHashMap remains empty, and then if consensusRatio is zero the early-return at line 54 is NOT triggered (because signatureHashMap.isEmpty() is true), causing the validator to throw SignatureVerificationException and halt ingestion of legitimate transactions as a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long integer overflow in the stake accumulation loop (line 67: stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by injecting signatures whose individual node stake values sum beyond Long.MAX_VALUE, causing stake to wrap to a negative value, making canReachConsensus() return false even when legitimate stake threshold is met, thereby blocking valid transaction records from being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature whose node returns a negative stake value, causing the stake accumulation at line 67 to undercount total stake for a hash group, so that canReachConsensus() returns false for a legitimately verified group, preventing valid transaction records from being accepted and causing a denial-of-service on transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature where getNode().getTotalStake() returns 0 as the first element, triggering the SignatureVerificationException at line 94 ('Invalid total staking weight'), causing the entire validate() call to abort and blocking all subsequent transaction record ingestion as a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft multiple StreamFileSignature objects all with the same fileHashAsHex value representing a tampered transaction record, each with a small individual stake but collectively exceeding the 1/3 threshold, so that canReachConsensus() returns true for the tampered hash group and the fraudulent transaction record is marked CONSENSUS_REACHED and ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that consensusCount > 0 (line 80) causes an immediate return after the first hash group reaches consensus, meaning that if a tampered hash group is iterated first by HashMultimap (non-deterministic ordering), it is accepted and the legitimate hash group is never evaluated, allowing a fraudulent transaction record to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status already set to CONSENSUS_REACHED (not VERIFIED), so that it is excluded from signatureHashMap at line 49-51, reducing the effective verified signature count below the consensus threshold and causing legitimate transaction records to fail validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects with mismatched fileHashAsHex values (each unique), so that each hash group has only one signature with insufficient stake, causing consensusCount to remain 0 and throwing SignatureVerificationException, thereby blocking ingestion of a legitimate transaction record batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable status of StreamFileSignature objects (line 72: s.setStatus(CONSENSUS_REACHED)) by passing the same signature object in multiple concurrent validate() calls, causing a race condition where a signature's status is mutated mid-validation, leading to incorrect consensus determination and acceptance of a tampered transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature collection where the first signature has a very large totalStake value (near Long.MAX_VALUE), causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a threshold that overflows BigDecimal arithmetic or produces an astronomically high required stake, making it impossible for any hash group to reach consensus and permanently blocking transaction record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a totalStake value that when multiplied by consensusRatio and rounded up via RoundingMode.CEILING produces a threshold of 1 (e.g., totalStake=1, consensusRatio=1/3), so that a single tampered signature with stake=1 passes canReachConsensus() and a fraudulent transaction record is accepted as having reached consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method takes totalStake exclusively from the first signature's node (line 45) and never validates consistency with other signatures' nodes' totalStake values, allowing an attacker to prepend a crafted low-totalStake signature to a legitimate collection and lower the consensus threshold for a tampered transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with a fileHash that is an empty byte array (length 0), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string '', which groups with other empty-hash signatures in signatureHashMap, allowing a tampered transaction record with an empty hash to falsely reach consensus if enough such signatures are injected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection where all signatures have status NOT_FOUND, causing signatureHashMap to be empty, and then if consensusRatio is non-zero the validator throws SignatureVerificationException, but if consensusRatio is zero the early-return is also blocked (isEmpty() check), effectively creating a condition where no transaction records can be ingested regardless of configuration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap's non-deterministic key iteration order (line 62) to ensure that a tampered hash group with just-sufficient stake is iterated before the legitimate hash group, causing the tampered group to be marked CONSENSUS_REACHED first and the validate() method to return early, accepting a fraudulent transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature whose getNode().getStake() returns Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the stake accumulation at line 67 to overflow to a negative value when combined with other stakes, making canReachConsensus() return false for a legitimate hash group and blocking valid transaction records from being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where each signature has a unique fileHashAsHex (all different tampered hashes), causing the signatureHashMap to have many single-entry groups, none of which individually meets the stake threshold, so that consensusCount remains 0 and a SignatureVerificationException is thrown, blocking all transaction record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not verify that all signatures in the collection refer to the same filename (only the first signature's filename is captured at line 44), allowing an attacker to mix signatures from different transaction record files in a single validate() call, causing cross-file consensus to be falsely reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with a fileHash that is a SHA-384 preimage collision of a legitimate transaction record's hash, causing the tampered signature to be grouped with legitimate signatures in signatureHashMap under the same hex key, inflating the stake count for the tampered hash group and causing a fraudulent transaction record to reach consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature collection where the first element's node returns a totalStake that is inconsistent with the actual network stake distribution, and since validate() never cross-checks totalStake against a trusted source, the attacker can set totalStake to a value that makes the 1/3 threshold trivially achievable with a single tampered signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusCount += validatedSignatures.size() accumulation at line 71, where consensusCount is a long that could theoretically overflow if an enormous number of signatures are injected, causing consensusCount to wrap to a negative value and the consensusCount > 0 check at line 80 to fail, blocking ingestion of legitimate transaction records?
